LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONERS PONIST LAW GROUP, P.C. AND SEAN E. PONIST'S PETITION TO APPOINT AN ARBITRATOR IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 3, 2019, A DAY THAT COUNSEL FOR RESPONDENTS IS APPARENTLY AVAILABLE. UNLESS THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO AN ARBITRATOR BEFORE THE HEARING, THE COURT INTENDS TO APPOINT ONE OF THE FIFTEEN PEOPLE LISTED IN PETITIONER'S INITIAL MEMORANDUM AS THE ARBITRATOR. ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 20, 2018 EACH SIDE MAY SEND AN EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG WITHOUT COPYING THE OTHER SIDE STRIKING UP TO SIX OF THE FIFTEEN PERSONS. ON JANUARY 3, 2019, THE COURT WILL ALSO FURTHER ADDRESS THE ISSUE OF SERVICE. ANY SUPPLEMENTAL OBJECTION PAPERS BY RESPONDENTS ON THIS ISSUE ARE DUE BY 12/7/18. ANY SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY PAPERS BY PETITIONERS ON THIS ISSUE ARE DUE BY 12/20/18. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUN-18-2020 HEARING RE: PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD [C.C.P. SECTION 1280 ET SEQ.] ON BEHALF OF NATIONWIDE BIWEEKLY ADMINISTRATION INC., LOAN PAYMENT ADMINISTRATION LLC, AND DANIEL S. LIPSKY, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PETITIONERS NATIONWIDE BIWEEKLY ADMINISTRATION, INC., LOAN PAYMENT ADMINISTRATION, LLC, AND DANIEL S. LIPSKY'S (COLLECTIVELY "LIPSKY") PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. THE COURT THEREFORE CONFIRMS THE AWARD IN FAVOR OF PONIST LAW GROUP, P.C. AND SEAN E. PONIST ("PONIST") PER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1285.2 AND 1286 AND JUDGMENT SHALL BE ENTERED IN PONIST'S FAVOR FORTHWITH. ORDER SIGNED AND SEPARATE JUDGMENT SIGNED. (SEE WORD MINUTES AND FORMAL ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

Amended Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award [C.C.P. Section 1280 Et Seq.] On Behalf Of Nationwide Biweekly Administration Inc., Loan Payment Administration Llc, And Daniel S. Lipsky
Off Calendar Jun-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD [C.C.P. SECTION 1280 ET SEQ.] ON BEHALF OF NATIONWIDE BIWEEKLY ADMINISTRATION INC., LOAN PAYMENT ADMINISTRATION LLC, AND DANIEL S. LIPSKY.

No appearances made by the parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Petitioners Nationwide Biweekly Administration, Inc., Loan Payment Administration, LLC, and Daniel S. Lipsky's (collectively "Lipsky") petition to vacate arbitration award is denied. The court therefore confirms the award in favor of Ponist Law Group, P.C. and Sean E. Ponist ("Ponist") per Code of Civil Procedure, 1285.2 and 1286 and judgment shall be entered in Ponist's favor forthwith. Order signed and separate judgment signed. (See Word minutes and formal order for complete ruling.)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award [C.C.P. S 1280 Et Seq] Of Nationwide Biweekly Administration, Inc., Loan Payment Administration, Llc, And Daniel S. Lipsky
Off Calendar Jun-12-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitration [Ccp Section 1281.6}; Mem Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph De Hope Jr., Esq. 925-238-3563 of Thompson Coe & O'Meara LLP for Petitioner PONIST LAW GROUP P.C., SEAN PONIST.
Suzanne Alves, Esq. 916-631-1546 of Alves Radcliffe LLP for Respondent Nationwide Biweekly through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views. Respondent argues service of process and non-compliance. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER PONIST LAW GROUP P.C., SEAN PONIST's Petition For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitration [Ccp Section 1281.6}.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Petitioners Ponist Law Group, P.C. and Sean E. Ponist's petition to appoint an arbitrator is granted. Marilyn O'Toole is appointed as the parties' arbitrator. The first backup arbitrator is Richard Idell. The second backup arbitrator is Bruce Methven. Respondents were properly served with the petition per CCP section 1290.4 (a) because the parties' agreement provides that arbitration shall be conducted under the rules of the AAA and rule 43 of the AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules provides that service of process may be effected by personal or mail delivery to the party or its representatives. Therefore, service on respondents' representatives in the arbitration proceedings by personal delivery and overnight mail or email constitutes proper service. All of the arguments in respondents' most recent memorandum lack merit and were either rejected or should have been raised at the November 29, 2018 hearing. Significantly, respondents fail to identify any AAA rules that might possibly be applicable that are in any way different than rule 43 of the AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitration [Ccp Section 1281.6}; Mem Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-29-2018 Continued To Jan-03-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. JOSEPH J. DE HOPE, JR., ESQ. of Thompson Coe & O'Meara, LLP (Tel: 925-238-3563) appearing for petitioners;
Atty. SUZANNE M. ALVES, ESQ. of Alves Radcliffe LLP (Tel: 916-631-1546) appearing via CourtCall for respondents.

Matter is argued by counsel for parties as to the issue of service. The Court rules as follows: Petitioners Ponist Law Group, P.C. and Sean E. Ponist's petition to appoint an arbitrator is continued to January 3, 2019, a day that counsel for respondents is apparently available. Unless the parties stipulate to an arbitrator before the hearing, the court intends to appoint one of the fifteen people listed in petitioner's initial memorandum as the arbitrator. On or before December 20, 2018 each side may send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org without copying the other side striking up to six of the fifteen persons. On January 3, 2019, the Court will also further address the issue of service. Any supplemental objection papers by respondents on this issue are due by 12/7/18. Any supplemental reply papers by petitioners on this issue are due by 12/20/18.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/HK)
